summary: Multiple security and correctness defects in auth.js.

findings.title: SQL injection in findUser
findings.body: username is concatenated directly into the SQL query string, allowing SQL injection.
findings.recommendation: Use a parameterized query with bound parameters.

findings.title: Hardcoded JWT secret
findings.body: JWT_SECRET is hard-coded in source; anyone with the repo can forge tokens.
findings.recommendation: Load the secret from an environment variable or secret store.

findings.title: Plaintext password comparison
findings.body: Passwords are compared in plaintext with ==; should hash with bcrypt and compare in constant time.
findings.recommendation: Store and compare a bcrypt/argon2 hash.

findings.title: Unguarded JSON.parse
findings.body: JSON.parse(raw) is unguarded and throws on malformed input, crashing parseSession.
findings.recommendation: Wrap JSON.parse in try/catch and validate the shape.
